 {"id":"ntm-2e9","title":"Implement stdin and --file support for ntm send","description":"Enable sending prompts from files and stdin pipes.

## What This Task Does

Extend ntm send to accept prompt content from:
1. --file flag: Read prompt from a file
2. stdin: When piped/redirected and no prompt argument given

## Use Cases

From file:
$ ntm send myproject --all --file prompts/review.md

From stdin (pipe):
$ cat error.log | ntm send myproject --cc 'Analyze this error:'
$ git diff | ntm send myproject --all 'Review these changes'

From stdin (redirect):
$ ntm send myproject --all < prompt.txt

Combined with prefix:
$ cat code.py | ntm send myproject --cc --prefix 'Review this code:\n'

## Technical Details

Detection logic:
1. If --file specified, read from file
2. Else if stdin is not a TTY, read from stdin
3. Else use positional prompt argument

Implementation:
func getPromptContent(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) (string, error) {
    if file != "" {
        content, err := os.ReadFile(file)
        return string(content), err
    }
    
    if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdin.Fd()) {
        content, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
        return string(content), err
    }
    
    if len(args) > 1 {
        return args[1], nil
    }
    
    return "", errors.New("no prompt provided")
}

New flags:
--file, -f    Read prompt from file
--prefix      Text to prepend to file/stdin content
--suffix      Text to append to file/stdin content

## Edge Cases

- Empty file: Error with helpful message
- Binary file: Warning, but proceed if forced
- Very large file: Stream rather than load all into memory? (future)
- Encoding: Assume UTF-8

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --file reads prompt from specified path
- [ ] Stdin detected and read when not TTY
- [ ] --prefix and --suffix work with file/stdin
- [ ] Clear error messages for missing file, empty content
- [ ] Existing behavior unchanged when neither used

-{"id":"ntm-2th5","title":"Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support (Codex, Gemini)","description":"# Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support

## What This Is
Extend rotation support from Claude-only to Codex and Gemini.

## Why This is Phase 4 (Not Earlier)
- Claude is the primary use case for the user
- Get Claude working perfectly first
- Codex and Gemini can be added incrementally

## Provider Differences

### Claude Code
- Login: `/login` command
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth patterns: "successfully logged in", "authenticated as"
- Continuation: "continue. Use ultrathink"

### Codex CLI
- Login: `/logout` then restart (no in-session re-auth!)
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth: Happens on startup
- **Key difference**: Codex ALWAYS uses restart strategy

### Gemini CLI
- Login: `/auth` command
- Exit: `/exit` or Ctrl+C
- Auth patterns: TBD (need to research)
- Continuation: TBD

## Implementation

### Provider Interface
```go
type Provider interface {
    Name() string
    LoginCommand() string
    ExitCommand() string
    AuthSuccessPatterns() []string
    ContinuationPrompt() string
    SupportsReauth() bool  // Codex returns false
}

var providers = map[string]Provider{
    "claude": &ClaudeProvider{},
    "codex":  &CodexProvider{},
    "gemini": &GeminiProvider{},
}
```

### Codex Specifics
```go
type CodexProvider struct{}

func (p *CodexProvider) SupportsReauth() bool {
    return false // Codex must always restart
}

func (p *CodexProvider) LoginCommand() string {
    return "/logout" // Then quit and restart
}
```

## Research Needed
- [ ] Gemini CLI auth flow
- [ ] Gemini CLI auth success patterns
- [ ] Gemini continuation prompt (if any)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `ntm rotate --pane=N` works for Codex panes
- [ ] `ntm rotate --pane=N` works for Gemini panes
- [ ] Provider auto-detected from pane title","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:15:59.839364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T17:08:47.390539-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-qisk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:02.100867-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-t7ol","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:02.722254-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-z3uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:26.950863-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-9f8k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:27.36387-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-2th5","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:20.866608-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-l5h","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure

## Background & Rationale

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) currently has very low test coverage across most packages. This EPIC addresses the need for:

1. **Full Unit Test Coverage** - Testing each function/method in isolation WITHOUT mocks or fakes where possible, using real dependencies
2. **Integration Tests** - Testing component interactions (CLI → tmux, config → commands, etc.)
3. **End-to-End (E2E) Tests** - Full workflow tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Current Coverage Analysis

| Package | Current Coverage | Status |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| cmd/ntm | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/cli | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/config | 16.2% | 🟡 Needs improvement |
| internal/output | 55.1% | 🟡 Good start |
| internal/palette | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/robot | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/status | 69.6% | 🟢 Good (has failures) |
| internal/tmux | 5.4% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tui/* | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tutorial | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/updater | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/watcher | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |

## Testing Philosophy

### No Mocks/Fakes Policy
- Use REAL tmux sessions for tmux tests (create/destroy per test)
- Use REAL file system for config tests
- Use REAL HTTP calls where applicable
- Only mock external services that are impractical (GitHub API rate limits, etc.)

### Detailed Logging Strategy
- All E2E tests output structured logs with timestamps
- Log file per test run with full command output
- Capture stderr/stdout separately
- Include system state before/after each test

## Success Criteria
- 80%+ coverage on all non-TUI packages
- 60%+ coverage on TUI packages (harder to test without mocks)
- All E2E tests pass on macOS and Linux
- Tests complete in <5 minutes total
- No flaky tests (3 consecutive passes required)

## Technical Considerations
- Tests requiring tmux must be skipped in CI without tmux
- Use Go's testing.Short() for quick vs full test modes
- Parallelize where safe (different tmux sessions)
- Clean up resources even on test failure","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:17:38.345103-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:48:43.676311-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-l5y","title":"Integrate beads into robot snapshot","description":"Include beads project status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Beads is how we track work. The snapshot needs to include bead summary so orchestrator knows what work is available, blocked, and in-progress.

## Integration Points
1. Detect if .beads/ directory exists
2. Run bd stats or equivalent query
3. Include summary counts and optionally ready list

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "beads": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "summary": {
      "total": 58,
      "open": 45,
      "in_progress": 3,
      "blocked": 15,
      "ready": 27,
      "closed": 13
    },
    "ready_preview": [
      {"id": "ntm-1rz", "title": "Create profiler package", "priority": "P0"},
      {"id": "ntm-7mg", "title": "Add design tokens", "priority": "P1"}
    ],
    "in_progress": [
      {"id": "ntm-abc", "title": "Implement feature X", "assigned": "BlueLake"}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Ready Preview Limit
- Default 5 items
- --robot-snapshot --bead-limit=10 to change
- Sorted by priority then age

## Graceful Degradation
```json
{"beads": {"available": false, "reason": "no .beads/ directory"}}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCastle","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:53:26.959482-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:58:54.711742-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:58:54.711742-05:00"}

-{"id":"ntm-l6pk","title":"Phase 5: Quota Display (Optional Enhancement)","description":"# Phase 5: Quota Display (Optional Enhancement)

## What This Is
Display current quota usage in dashboard and CLI. This is a **nice-to-have** feature, not essential for rotation to work.

## Why Optional?
The core rotation feature (Phases 1-3) works without knowing quota percentages. Users will:
1. Hit rate limit
2. Get notified
3. Run `ntm rotate`

Quota display is polish that helps users proactively manage accounts before hitting limits.

## Implementation: Use CLI Commands via tmux

### Approach (No PTY Library Needed!)
```go
// Send /usage to pane, capture output, parse
func QueryQuota(paneID string) (*QuotaInfo, error) {
    // 1. Send command
    if err := tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "/usage", true); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // 2. Wait for output
    time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
    
    // 3. Capture and parse
    output, err := tmux.CapturePaneOutput(paneID, 50)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    return parseClaudeUsage(output)
}
```

### Parsing Claude /usage Output
```go
type QuotaInfo struct {
    SessionPercent float64
    WeeklyPercent  float64
    ResetTime      string
    RawOutput      string
}

var usagePatterns = map[string]*regexp.Regexp{
    "session": regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)session[^:]*:\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%`),
    "weekly":  regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)weekly[^:]*:\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%`),
    "reset":   regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)reset[^:]*:\s*(.+)`),
}

func parseClaudeUsage(output string) (*QuotaInfo, error) {
    info := &QuotaInfo{RawOutput: output}
    
    if m := usagePatterns["session"].FindStringSubmatch(output); m != nil {
        info.SessionPercent, _ = strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
    }
    if m := usagePatterns["weekly"].FindStringSubmatch(output); m != nil {
        info.WeeklyPercent, _ = strconv.ParseFloat(m[1], 64)
    }
    if m := usagePatterns["reset"].FindStringSubmatch(output); m != nil {
        info.ResetTime = strings.TrimSpace(m[1])
    }
    
    return info, nil
}
```

### CLI Command: `ntm quota`
```
$ ntm quota myproject

Pane  Provider  Session  Weekly  Reset
────  ────────  ───────  ──────  ─────
0     Claude    45%      72%     Mon 00:00 UTC
1     Claude    45%      72%     Mon 00:00 UTC
2     Codex     -        -       -
```

### Dashboard Integration (Optional)
Add quota column to pane list:
```
│ 0 │ Claude │ working │ [███████░░░] 72% │
```

## Considerations
- Sending /usage interrupts the agent - only do on demand, not continuously
- Cache results to avoid re-querying
- Make this opt-in, not default behavior

## Dependencies
- Phase 1-3 complete (rotation works)
- Not blocking rotation functionality

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `ntm quota` shows quota for Claude panes
- [ ] Dashboard can show quota (toggle)
- [ ] Doesn't interrupt active agent work","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:16:49.385291-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:33:54.204491-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-l6pk","depends_on_id":"ntm-qisk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:02.885686-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l6pk","depends_on_id":"ntm-t7ol","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:03.07791-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l6pk","depends_on_id":"ntm-2th5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:03.440646-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l6pk","depends_on_id":"ntm-0kfe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:19:27.694884-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-l6pk","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:35:20.943562-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-laa","title":"EPIC: Resilience & Reliability","description":"Make NTM self-healing: detect failures, recover automatically, and notify users.

## Problem Statement

When running multiple AI agents over extended periods, failures are inevitable:

1. **Agent Crashes**: Agents can crash, exit unexpectedly, or hang
2. **Rate Limits**: API rate limits cause temporary failures (429 errors)
3. **Silent Failures**: Users don't notice problems until manually checking
4. **Manual Recovery**: Every failure requires human intervention to restart
5. **Lost Work**: Crashed agents lose context and must start fresh

Currently, NTM provides no visibility into agent health and no recovery mechanisms.
Users must constantly monitor tmux panes to catch problems.

## Goals

1. Detect agent health issues (crashes, rate limits, errors, hangs)
2. Automatically restart crashed agents with configurable policies
3. Implement smart backoff for rate-limited agents
4. Push notifications when events occur (desktop, webhook, Slack)
5. Provide ntm health command for manual health checks

## Why This Matters

Reliability is the difference between a toy and a tool. Users need to trust that:
- If they start 4 agents at night, they'll still be running in the morning
- Problems are detected and reported, not silently ignored
- Recovery happens automatically when possible
- They're notified when intervention is truly needed

This transforms NTM from 'launch and babysit' to 'launch and trust'.

## Success Criteria

- ntm health shows status of all agents with clear indicators
- --auto-restart flag enables automatic crash recovery
- Rate limit detection triggers backoff, not crash
- Notifications fire on configurable events
- Agents survive transient failures without user intervention

## Technical Approach

Health detection via tmux output analysis:
- Process status: tmux list-panes shows if command is running
- Activity monitoring: Compare pane content over time
- Error patterns: Regex match for known error signatures
- Idle detection: Prompt pattern visible = waiting for input

Recovery mechanisms:
- Restart: Re-run agent command in same pane
- Backoff: Exponential delay on rate limit detection
- Limits: Max restart count to prevent infinite loops
- Alerting: Notify after N failures

## Notification Channels

- Desktop: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- Webhook: HTTP POST to configurable URL (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell: Run custom command with event data
- Log: Append to file for later review

## Relation to ntm-4pp (Observability)

ntm-4pp focuses on OBSERVABILITY: seeing what agents are doing.
This epic focuses on RELIABILITY: keeping agents running.

They are complementary:
- ntm-4pp: 'Agent 3 hit a rate limit' (observation)
- This epic: 'Agent 3 hit a rate limit, backing off 60s, will retry' (action)

## Configuration

[resilience]
auto_restart = true
max_restarts = 3
restart_delay_seconds = 30
rate_limit_backoff = true
backoff_multiplier = 2.0
max_backoff_seconds = 300

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.restarted", "session.unhealthy"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/..."
